what_it_does: This file is a metadata record for a channel attachment: it links an attachment identifier to a channel, an attachment type, a board-item target, and an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
Target is a board:// URI pointing to item-9027a812
Added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone (2026-06-24T01:32:20.803901+00:00)
standouts: File contains only metadata, no payload or description
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of metadata fields
References external resources with a URI scheme (board://)
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit timezone
Stores one attachment record per markdown file
concerns: No payload, summary, or description of what is attached
No modification history or versioning fields
The meaning/content of the referenced board item is not present
notes: A bare attachment metadata stub that points to a board item but carries no attachment content or context.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
What is the board item item-9027a812 about?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is board_items a type for all board attachments or only a specific category?
purpose_vs_actual: The fields indicate it is meant to record an attachment linked to a board item, and that is exactly what the metadata does; no mismatch.
intention_signals: It appears designed to create a durable, referenceable association between a channel attachment and a specific board item, likely for retrieval, audit, or downstream fusion.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI scheme in the target field is the most distinctive feature; otherwise it is a conventional metadata record.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, timestamped instance of an attachment-to-board-item relationship, typed as board_items, within the dragnet-development channel.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment metadata record (id att-de8a01d2) in the dragnet-development channel, typed as board_items, targeting board item item-9027a812 and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to persistently link an attachment to a board item for retrieval and audit within the channel memory ledger.
